headline: Exxon, Chevron, big oil send signals ahead of earnings
summary: For the most part, 2026 has been a very good year for oil giants and the not-so-giant oil companies. Oil prices are up substantially since the start of the year: 67% for light sweet crude, the benchmark U.S. oil, and 78% for Brent, the global benchmark. Which means, of course, that more of the ...

headline: Exxon and Chevron Stock Jump. BP Doubles Profit on ‘Exceptional’ Oil Trading.
summary: BP  stock rose Tuesday after the rally in crude prices helped the British oil major to double its first-quarter profit.  BP’s American depositary receipts climbed 1% to $46.40.  The results cover a period where the Iran war disrupted shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, sparking a surge in oil prices.

headline: Warren Buffett Owns 3 Strong Buy Dividend Aristocrat Giants—1 for Almost 40 Years
summary: Warren Buffett stepped down as chief executive of Berkshire Hathaway (NYSE: BRK-B) on December 31, 2025, after six decades leading the conglomerate he transformed from a struggling textile mill into a $1 trillion empire. The “Oracle of Omaha” left his successor, Greg Abel, with a very concentrated portfolio: more than 65% of Berkshire’s $381 billion portfolio ... headline: BP Stock Rises After Huge Jump in Profit. What It Means for Exxon and Chevron.
summary: BP  stock was rising on Tuesday after the rally in crude prices helped the British oil major to double its first-quarter profit.  BP’s American depositary receipts climbed 3.7% to $47.65 ahead of the opening bell.  The results cover a period where the war in the Middle East disrupted shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, sparking a surge in oil prices.
